name: Ayush Makwana
title: AskCricket
reason: Genuinely agentic: src/agent.js implements a multi-round (MAX_ROUNDS=5) async-generator loop that calls Azure OpenAI gpt-5.4-mini Responses API with six well-defined tools (get_player_stats, compare_players, find_matches, match_context, clutch_index, head_to_head) over a 34k-delivery IPL dataset, with tool_choice='required' on round 0 to force grounding. Cloudflare Worker streams responses via SSE. README is thorough (architecture diagram, tools table, setup). Demo at askcricket.ayushmakwana.com returns 200. Custom domain, structured JSON answer card with chart hints. Cleanly fits Data & Insights brief and the originality is real (clutch_index composite, phase-aware splits). One of the strongest batch-5 entries.
ai_evidence: Azure OpenAI Responses API agent loop in src/agent.js with 6 function-calling tools in src/tools.js; src/azure.js wraps callAzureChat

name: Dwij Joshi
title: Nova Habit Tracker
reason: Most complete entry in the batch. Next.js + TypeScript + Firebase/Firestore with proper structure (src/app routes for galaxy, focus, coach, battles, achievements; components split into gamification/habits/social/ai/wellness). Real Gemini integration via @google/generative-ai SDK in src/app/api/chat/route.ts — stateful chat with user-context system prompt (level, XP, habits), conversation history, error handling. Live Vercel demo returned 200. Strong gamified-habit fit: XP/levels, focus rooms, leaderboard, habit battles, AI coach 'Nova'. Cosmic-RPG framing is genuinely original. Minor knocks: README is the default create-next-app boilerplate; AI is one chat endpoint rather than multi-step agent loop.
ai_evidence: @google/generative-ai SDK; GoogleGenerativeAI + getGenerativeModel('gemini-pro') with multi-turn chat history in src/app/api/chat/route.ts

name: Hadin Tai
title: CrickIntel AI
reason: Genuinely agentic submission. backend/requirements.txt declares crewai, langchain-google-genai, langchain-groq, chromadb. backend/agents/ has insight_agent.py, commentary_agent.py, chatbot_agent.py — each instantiating CrewAI Agents with distinct roles (Match Analyst, Fan Engagement, etc.) on Gemini 1.5 Flash and Groq Llama. backend/analytics/ does deterministic ball-by-ball stats first, then hands off to LLMs (hybrid architecture as claimed). FastAPI backend + React/Vite frontend with Recharts; 76 files, clean README with setup. Only weakness is no hosted demo URL — but the architecture is clearly the strongest in the batch.
ai_evidence: crewai + langchain-google-genai + langchain-groq in requirements.txt; multi-agent CrewAI definitions in backend/agents/insight_agent.py, commentary_agent.py, chatbot_agent.py

name: Pratham Raval
title: CRIQ
reason: Rolled-back HEAD (`Remove sensitive API key values from README`) already has the full stack present at the 23:49 deadline: backend/ FastAPI (main.py 177 LOC with 10+ routes — matches, summary, pressure, analysis, player stats, wagon wheel, matchup) plus backend/data.py (1041 LOC pandas analytics over IPL matches.csv/deliveries.csv); frontend/ React 18 + Vite + TS + Tailwind + d3 + recharts + zustand with pages (Home, MatchSummary, MatchAnalysis, Player, Team, FanTools) and 13 components (FanIQQuiz, WagonWheel, MomentumChart, PressureBar, WinProbChart, StatExplainer). frontend/src/utils/ai.ts wraps Gemini 2.0 Flash with caching and intelligent mock fallbacks, called from 5 surfaces (Home, MatchSummary, FanIQQuiz, MomentumChart, StatExplainer). Detailed 10KB README with quickstart. No live demo URL. Even after dropping 8 post-event commits, the in-window state is a polished, theme-fitting submission — not materially weaker than originally described.
ai_evidence: Gemini 2.0 Flash REST integration in frontend/src/utils/ai.ts with cache + mock fallback; consumed by FanIQQuiz.tsx, MomentumChart.tsx, StatExplainer.tsx, Home.tsx, MatchSummary.tsx; backend .env reads GEMINI_API_KEY (python-dotenv)

name: Patel Jaykumar Mahendrabhai
title: NeuroSport
reason: Highest-scoring entry in the batch. Flutter app (web + android + ios + desktop) with a substantial Gemini 2.5 Flash integration in lib/ai/gemini_service.dart — streaming commentary (typewriter effect), win-probability estimation, fan-reaction roasting, prediction rating, plus a scenario simulator and fantasy assistant screen. Uses google_generative_ai SDK with structured prompt builders, integrates Cricbuzz API for live data. Multi-screen architecture (match_analysis, scenario_simulator, fan_zone, fantasy_assistant) shows breadth. README is thoughtful and problem-framed. No live URL and the form admits the AI 'may not work as the limit is reached'.
ai_evidence: google_generative_ai (Gemini 2.5 Flash) in lib/ai/gemini_service.dart — generateContent + generateContentStream; secrets in lib/core/secrets.dart; used across match_analysis, scenario_simulator, fan_zone, live_commentary_ticker, app_providers

name: Jagat Jeevan Mahapatra
title: CricIQ - AI
reason: TanStack Start app generated via Lovable+Replit. Two server functions wire up Gemini 2.0 Flash: src/fns/gemini.ts (chat) and src/fns/ai-analysis.ts (match analysis), both using process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY and the v1beta REST endpoint with proper error handling and typed interfaces. Reasonable route structure (_app/dashboard, fantasy, explain, insights, matches, players, teams). Lovable demo (ball-wise-insight.lovable.app) returns 200; the Replit URL 404s. Code quality is decent but obviously scaffolded — heavy shadcn/ui component dump, mock-data.ts suggests not all features are live-data driven. Fit is good for Data & Insights but pitch line ('give me one') signals low effort on submission polish. Originality average — yet another cricket insights dashboard.
ai_evidence: process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY; fetch to generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-2.0-flash in src/fns/gemini.ts and src/fns/ai-analysis.ts

name: Modi Krish
title: ProduX
reason: Live demo at produx-orcin.vercel.app returns 200. Most substantial codebase in the batch: full MERN with Socket.io realtime, Mongoose models (User, Habit, Task, Friendship, Hobby, Message), separate controllers/routes for auth/habit/dashboard/gamification/social, JWT cookies, plus a manifest-v3 Chrome extension for 'Focus Guard'. Habit tracking, XP/levels, 21-day challenges, badges — squarely on-brief. But zero AI in product: no openai/anthropic/gemini/groq/genai in either client or server package.json, and the form itself admits AI was only used to 'translate vision to architecture' during dev. The Chrome-extension-as-accountability angle is a genuinely unusual axis.
ai_evidence: no AI code in product — server/client package.json contain no LLM SDK; AI claimed only as a dev-time architecture aid

name: Aarya Patel
title: CricBrain
reason: Solid fullstack MERN with a real Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite integration in server/controllers/aiController.js — three distinct endpoints (commentary, insights, ELI5 explanation), persona-switching prompts, JSON-mode for structured outputs, and graceful mock-data fallbacks. Client/server split is clean (routes, controllers, data). 'Sledge Mode' (Analyst vs Fanatic personas) and Beginner Mode tooltips are genuinely fan-friendly framings. No live demo URL is the main weakness, and the AI flow is essentially one-shot calls rather than an agent loop. README is good and run instructions are clear.
ai_evidence: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite via @google/generative-ai in server/controllers/aiController.js — generateCommentary, generateInsights (JSON mode), generateExplanation; gemini config in server/config/gemini.js

name: Shivansh Darji
title: Habit Warrior
reason: Genuinely creative angle — every habit is summoned as a 3D monster (slime/orc/dragon), and completing a habit literally strikes the enemy in a Three.js arena. Live GitHub Pages demo loads (200). Vanilla JS but well-decomposed (HabitStore / Game / UI layers across game.js, habits.js, ui.js, tracker.js, analytics.js, charts.js, sound.js), with transparent combat math, achievements, streak combo bonuses, and a WebAudio SFX synth. README is exceptional. The killer flaw for an Agentic-AI hackathon: no AI at all — 'as builder' just means Cursor authored it. Pure originality + execution carry the score.
ai_evidence: no AI code found; Cursor was used as code editor only

name: Darshit Kotecha
title: Habit Quest / EpicDaily
reason: Cricket-themed habit tracker built with Vite/React + Express. server.ts:4 imports @google/genai and exposes /api/habit-tips that calls gemini-flash-latest for daily motivational tips (real single LLM call with caching + fallback). App.tsx is 1163 lines with real screens (Nets dashboard, Coach Academy, Career Path, Stadium). README is polished with screenshots and two AI Studio Cloud Run URLs (preview returns 302). Themed gamification (cricket form, debut→legend) is creative. AI is light (one tips endpoint) rather than truly agentic, but the build itself is substantial.
ai_evidence: @google/genai in package.json; gemini-flash-latest call in server.ts:22

name: Yash Patel
title: Habitquest
reason: MERN-ish backend (Express + Mongoose) with a real tool-calling agent in backend/controllers/aiController.js (230 lines): uses groq-sdk with a tools array (get_active_habits, create_habit, etc.) and a while-loop over response.tool_calls — genuinely agentic flow letting the chatbot create/edit/complete habits via function calling. Frontend (Vite+React) has Dashboard, HabitManagement, Focus, Leaderboard, Achievements, AICoach component, and ranks utility (gamification). 77 files, auth context, JWT middleware. No live demo URL and no top-level README, but the Groq tool-calling agent is the most legitimately agentic gamified-habit entry in the batch.
ai_evidence: groq-sdk in backend/package.json; tool-calling loop with multi-tool definitions in backend/controllers/aiController.js

name: Deep Vadher
title: LifeQuest AI
reason: Live demo at lifequestai.netlify.app/login returns 200. Project is visually polished — many pages (quests, world, boss, coach, skilltree, futureself, domain, stats, shadow mode, cinematic intro), heavy framer-motion, clean app-router structure. But the 'AI' is fake: app/coach/page.js ships a hardcoded aiResponses object keyed by intent (procrastination/motivation/habits/...), and `grep -rn 'fetch\|axios'` across the entire app/ folder returns zero hits. No openai/gemini/anthropic SDK in package.json. So all 'AI narration' is canned strings. Strong cinematic UX and habit framing earn it solid fit + originality, but agentic score has to be near 1.
ai_evidence: no AI code — aiResponses object in app/coach/page.js is hardcoded literal strings, zero fetch/axios calls in app/

name: Kuldeepsinh Jhala
title: Levelup AI
reason: Three-service layout: BE (Express/SQLite habits API), BE-AI (separate Express AI proxy), FE (React+Vite+TS). BE-AI/ai/gemini.js calls @google/generative-ai with a multi-key rotation pool (up to 50 keys) for quota handling — production-minded touch. BE proxies via services/aiProxy.js to BE-AI for /ask-gemini. Real controllers (auth, habits, onboarding, ai, motivation), Mongoose/SQLite models, schemas, prompts/ folder. 121 files total. AI generates habit suggestions and a 'lifespan pending' dashboard from user data — creative framing. No live demo URL and no top-level README; AI flow is single-call rather than multi-agent.
ai_evidence: @google/generative-ai used in BE-AI/ai/gemini.js (multi-key Gemini 2.0 Flash); proxied via BE/src/services/aiProxy.js

name: Riyank Singh
title: IPL GROUND INTELLIGENCE
reason: Best Data & Insights fit in the batch — real Cricsheet ball-by-ball ingestion via backend/app/parser.py building venue_intelligence (powerplay runs/wickets, toss→win correlation) served by FastAPI through backend/app/api/routes.py and backend/app/services/analytics_service.py to a React/Tailwind frontend. Clean separation (core/datastore, utils/loader, services). requirements.txt is FastAPI+pydantic only, no LLM libs. Form is refreshingly honest: AI was used as a 'syntax compiler / vibe coding' during dev, not at runtime — so the agentic score has to reflect that. Demo is a Google Drive video (200). Ground-bias angle is a less-trodden cricket-analytics axis.
ai_evidence: no AI runtime — only FastAPI/pydantic/uvicorn in requirements.txt; submitter explicitly states AI was a dev-time tool

name: FarhaanAli Vohra
title: Apple
reason: Backend-heavy MERN with a real Nebius (DeepSeek V3.2) call in src/services/nlpService.js — parses free-text habit logs into structured {sentiment, effort, durationMinutes, focus, obstacles, tags} JSON, with a heuristic fallback if the API key is missing. Medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold log tiers) via Mongoose models is an unusual, mature touch. Repo only appears to contain the server (no client UI in the snapshot), which weakens the gamification claim — the XP/level mechanic referenced in the form is not visible in code. No demo URL.
ai_evidence: Nebius DeepSeek V3.2 fetch call in src/services/nlpService.js (chat/completions endpoint, JSON-only system prompt); config in src/config.js

name: Zishan Vhora
title: Lockin
reason: By far the most product-complete habit builder in the batch: React + TanStack Start + Supabase (Auth/RLS/migrations), Radix UI, doodle aesthetic, Counter & Timer habit types, Shield system to protect streaks, Battleground 1v1 duels, achievements, friends, profile pages — 75 src files plus three SQL migrations. Strong README with clear setup. No live demo URL submitted, which hurts. Importantly, no LLM/agentic code exists — the 'agentic' claim refers solely to using AI (Antigravity/Claude/Stitch/Lovable) to write the app, which the rubric does not credit. Great Gamified Habit Builder fit, weak Agentic-theme fit.
ai_evidence: no AI code found; @supabase/supabase-js is the only external service; 'Vibe Coding' was used as the build methodology, not a runtime feature

name: Daksh Thakurani
title: Streaksmith
reason: Easily the most polished engineering in the batch — Next.js 16 + Prisma 7 + Postgres (Neon) + Bun, with proper auth (sessions, password lib), and well-isolated lib/ modules for streak, coins, badges, missions, habit-completion, dashboard, catalog. Habits/streaks/coins/badges/rewards all present — perfect Gamified-Habit-Builder fit. Demo-user bypass for evaluators is a thoughtful touch. The fatal weakness for an Agentic-AI hackathon: zero AI in the codebase, and the form admits AI was only used for architecting. No live URL, only docs.
ai_evidence: no AI code found; form states 'used open code for architecture' only

name: Dhruv Mahant
title: Data & Insights
reason: Solid Express/TypeScript backend (sportlens-ai) with auth, models (Match/Player/Team/User/Analytics), controllers, services (ai, cache, impact, insight, momentum, prediction) and Dockerfile/docker-compose. Real OpenAI integration in src/services/ai.service.ts calling gpt-4o-mini, invoked from insight.service.ts with prompt-hash caching. No live demo URL submitted and form pitch is one bland line ('A stop solution for easy analysis of data'). Pure backend with no UI shown, so the user-facing 'insights' story is unproven. Architecture is professional but the AI usage is a single chat-completion call, not multi-step agentic.
ai_evidence: openai SDK in src/config/openai.ts; chat.completions.create with gpt-4o-mini in src/services/ai.service.ts; called from insight.service.ts:64 and :102

name: Kalpya Shah
title: habitforge
reason: Live Vercel demo (https://habitforges-red.vercel.app, 200 OK) is the strongest asset. Codebase has ~15 React components (Login, TopHUD, HabitCard, Vault, AddHabitModal, HeroProfile, Analytics, RightCanvas with a canvasEngine util) and uses localStorage for persistence — a real attempt at gamified UI, not a template. However: AI was only used at authoring time (Gemini for prompts, Antigravity as editor) with no runtime AI integration, and the README is the unmodified Vite+React starter. Originality is low — generic XP/vault/hero framing without a distinct hook.
ai_evidence: no AI runtime code; form mentions AI only as coding assistant (Gemini for brainstorming, Antigravity as editor)

Backend talks to Cricbuzz via RapidAPI (src/lib/cricbuzz.functions.ts) for real cricket data, but there is no LLM call anywhere in the code. Marketing copy on stadium-scope-pro.lovable.app (200 OK) advertises '5,200+ AI Insights/Day' and '98% Prediction Accuracy', neither of which is backed by the codebase. Form 'agentic' answer ('To Get and to Give Efficient prompt') is itself near-empty. Visually impressive, technically not agentic.

All match data is static mock JSON. UI exists (Dashboard, PlayerAnalytics, FloatingChat) and looks polished, but the 'AI analyst' is a sham. No live demo URL.
